author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “To every careful thinker there is a gain” ( Prov. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23 , LXX.), saith the proverb; and Christ implied more than this, when He said, “He that seeketh findeth.” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it does not occur to me any more to wonder how Philip followed Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Andrew was persuaded when he had heard from John, and Peter the same from Andrew, but Philip not having learned anything from any but Christ who said to him only this, “Follow Me,” straightway obeyed, and went not back, but even became a preacher to others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he ran to Nathanael and said to him, “We have found Him of whom Moses in the Law and the Prophets did write.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou what a thoughtful mind he had, how assiduously he meditated on the writings of Moses, and expected the Advent? for the expression, “we have found,” belongs always to those who are in some way seeking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The day following Jesus went forth into Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Before any had joined Him, He called no one; and He acted thus not without cause, but according to his own wisdom and intelligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if, when no one came to Him spontaneously, He had Himself drawn them, they might perhaps have started away; but now, having chosen this of themselves, they afterwards remained firm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He calls Philip, one who was better acquainted with Him; for he, as having been born and bred in Galilee, knew Him more than others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having then taken the disciples, He next goes to the capture of the others, and draws to Him Philip and Nathanael.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now in the case of Nathanael this was not so wonderful, because the fame of Jesus had gone forth into all Syria. ( Matt. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the wonderful thing was respecting Peter and James and Philip, that they believed, not only before the miracles, but that they did so being of Galilee, out of which “ariseth no prophet,” nor “can any good thing come”; for the Galilæans were somehow of a more boorish and dull disposition than others; but even in this Christ displayed forth His power, by selecting from a land which bore no fruit His choicest disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is then probable that Philip having seen Peter and Andrew, and having heard what John had said, followed; and it is probable also that the voice of Christ wrought in him somewhat; for He knew those who would be serviceable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But all these points the Evangelist cuts short.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That Christ should come, he knew; that this was Christ, he knew not, and this I say that he heard either from Peter or John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But John mentions his village also, that you may learn that “God hath chosen the weak things of the world.” ( 1 Cor. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found Him of whom Moses in the Law and the Prophets did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He says this, to make his preaching credible, which it must be if it rests on Moses and the Prophets besides, and by this to abash his hearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since Nathanael was an exact man, and one who viewed all things with truth, as Christ also testified and the event showed, Philip with reason refers him to Moses and the Prophets, that so he might receive Him who was preached.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he not troubled though he called Him “the son of Joseph”; for still he was supposed to be his son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whence, O Philip, is it plain that this is He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What proof dost thou mention to us? for it is not enough merely to assert this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What sign hast thou seen, what miracle?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not without danger is it to believe without cause in such matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What proof then hast thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The same as Andrew,” he replies; for he though unable to produce the wealth which he had found, or to describe his treasure in words, when he had discovered it, led his brother to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So too did Philip.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How this is the Christ, and how the prophets proclaimed Him beforehand, he said not; but he draws him to Jesus, as knowing that he would not afterwards fall off, if he should once taste His words and teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Philip saith unto him, Come and see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to Him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He praises and approves the man, because he had said, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?” and yet he ought to have been blamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely not; for the words are not those of an unbeliever, nor deserving blame, but praise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How so, and in what way?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because Nathanael had considered the writings of the Prophets more than Philip.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he had heard from the Scriptures, that Christ must come from Bethlehem, and from the village in which David was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This belief at least prevailed among the Jews, and the Prophet had proclaimed it of old, saying, “And thou, Bethlehem, art by no means the least among the princes of Judah, for out of thee shall come a Governor, that shall feed My people Israel.” ( Matt. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so when he heard that He was “from Nazareth,” he was confounded, and doubted, not finding the announcement of Philip to agree with the prediction of the Prophet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe his wisdom and candor even in his doubting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He did not at once say, “Philip, thou deceivest me, and speakest falsely, I believe thee not, I will not come; I have learned from the prophets that Christ must come from Bethlehem, thou sayest ‘from Nazareth’; therefore this is not that Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said nothing like this; but what does he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He goes to Him himself; showing, by not admitting that Christ was “of Nazareth,” his accuracy respecting the Scriptures, and a character not easily deceived; and by not rejecting him who brought the tidings, the great desire which he felt for the coming of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he thought within himself that Philip was probably mistaken about the place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe, I pray you, his manner of declining, how gentle he has made it, and in the form of a question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he said not, “Galilee produces no good”; but how said he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Philip also was very prudent; for he is not as one perplexed, angry, and annoyed, but perseveres, wishing to bring over the man, and manifesting to us from the first of his preaching the firmness which becomes an Apostle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also Christ saith, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that there is such a person as a false Israelite; but this is not such an one; for his judgment, Christ saith, is impartial, he speaks nothing from favor, or from ill-feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Jews, when they were asked where Christ should be born, replied, “In Bethlehem” ( Matt. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), and produced the evidence, saying, “And thou, Bethlehem, art by no means the least among the princes of Judah.” ( Mic. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Before they had seen Him they bore this witness, but when they saw Him in their malice they concealed the testimony, saying, “But as for this fellow, we know not whence He is.” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nathanael did not so, but continued to retain the opinion which he had from the beginning, that He was not “of Nazareth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then do the prophets call Him a Nazarene?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From His being brought up and abiding there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He omits to say, “I am not ‘of Nazareth,’ as Philip hath told thee, but of Bethlehem,” that He may not at once make the account seem questionable; and besides this, because, even if He had gained belief, He would not have given sufficient proof that He was the Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what hindered Him without being Christ, from being of Bethlehem, like the others who were born there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then He omits; but He does that which has most power to bring him over, for He shows that He was present when they were conversing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Nathanael had said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whence knowest Thou me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He replies, “Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe a man firm and steady.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When Christ had said, “Behold an Israelite indeed,” he was not made vain by this approbation, he ran not after this open praise, but continues seeking and searching more exactly, and desires to learn something certain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He still enquired as of a man, but Jesus answered as God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He said, “I have known thee from the first,” (him and the candor of his character, this He knew not as a man, from having closely followed him, but as God from the first,) “and but now I saw thee by the fig-tree”; when there was no one present there but only Philip and Nathanael who said all these things in private.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is mentioned, that having seen him afar off, He said, “Behold an Israelite indeed”; to show, that before Philip came near, Christ spoke these words, that the testimony might not be suspected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason also He named the time, the place, and the tree; because if He had only said, “Before Philip came to thee, I saw thee,” He might have been suspected of having sent him, and of saying nothing wonderful; but now, by mentioning both the place where he was when addressed by Philip, and the name of the tree, and the time of the conversation, He showed that His foreknowledge was unquestionable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He did not merely show to him His foreknowledge, but instructed him also in another way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He brought him to a recollection of what they then had said; as, “Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it was most especially on this account that Nathanael received Him, because when he had uttered these words, He did not condemn, but praised and approved him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he was assured that this was indeed the Christ, both from His foreknowledge, and from His having exactly searched out his sentiments, which was the act of One who would show that He knew what was in his mind; and besides, from His not having blamed, but rather praised him when he had seemed to speak against Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said then, that Philip had “called” him; but what Philip had said to him or he to Philip, He omitted, leaving it to his own conscience, and not desiring farther to rebuke him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was it then only “before Philip called him” that He “saw” him? did He not see him before this with His sleepless eye?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saw him, and none could gainsay it; but this is what it was needful to say at the time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what did Nathanael?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he had received an unquestionable proof of His foreknowledge, he hastened to confess Him, showing by his previous delay his caution, and his fairness by his assent afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, said the Evangelist,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He answered and saith unto Him, Rabbi, Thou art the Son of God, Thou art the King of Israel:”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how his soul is filled at once with exceeding joy, and embraces Jesus with words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou art,” saith he, “that expected, that sought-for One.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how he is amazed, how he marvels? how he leaps and dances with delight?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So ought we also to rejoice, who have been thought worthy to know the Son of God; to rejoice, not in thought alone, but to show it also by our actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what must they do who rejoice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Obey Him who has been made known to them; and they who obey, must do whatever He willeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if we are going to do what angers Him, how shall we show that we rejoice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See ye not in our houses when a man entertains one whom he loves, how gladly he exerts himself, running about in every direction, and though it be needful to spend all that he has, sparing nothing so that he please his visitor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if one who invites should not attend to his guest, and not do such things as would procure him ease, though he should say ten thousand times that he rejoices at his coming, he could never be believed by him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And justly; for this should be shown by actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, since Christ hath come to us, show that we rejoice, and do nothing that may anger him; let us garnish the abode to which He has come, for this they do who rejoice; let us set before Him the meal which He desires to eat, for this they do who hold festival.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is this meal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith Himself; “My meat is, that I may do the will of Him that sent me.” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He is hungry, let us feed Him; when He is thirsty, let us give Him drink: though thou give Him but a cup of cold water, He receives it; for He loves thee, and to one who loves, the offerings of the beloved, though they be small, appear great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Only be not thou slothful; though thou cast in but two farthings, He refuses them not, but receives them as great riches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He is without wants, and receives these offerings, not because He needs them, it is reasonable that all distinction should be not in the quantity of the gifts, but the intention of the giver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Only show that thou lovest Him who is come, that for His sake thou art giving all diligence, that thou rejoicest at His coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See how He is disposed toward thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He came for thee, He laid down His life for thee, and after all this He doth not refuse even to entreat thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We are ambassadors,” saith Paul, “for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us.” ( 2 Cor. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And who is so mad,” saith some one, “as not to love his own Master?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I say so too, and I know that not one of us would deny this in words or intention; but one who is beloved desires love to be shown, not by words only, but by deeds also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to say that we love, and not to act like lovers, is ridiculous, not only before God, but even in the sight of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then to confess Him in word only, while in deeds we oppose Him, is not only unprofitable, but also hurtful to us; let us, I entreat you, also make confession by our works; that we also may obtain a confession from Him in that day, when before His Father He shall confess those who are worthy in Christ Jesus our Lord, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
